channel.title: Business - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

1. SpaceX’s Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO  TechCrunch
  2. American Airlines picks SpaceX's Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi on more than 500 planes  CNBC
  3. Delta Could Have Had the World’s Best Wi-Fi. It Chose Something Else Instead  inc.com
  4. American Airlines stock jumps as the airline picks Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi service  Yahoo Finance
  5. Starlink WiFi coming to 500+ American Airlines planes  Mashable

item.title: Congress Is Unlikely To Prevent A New Federal Ban On Hemp THC Products This Year, Top Marijuana Reform Group Says - Marijuana Moment
item.description:
1. Congress Is Unlikely To Prevent A New Federal Ban On Hemp THC Products This Year, Top Marijuana Reform Group Says  Marijuana Moment

  5. State Hemp Policy Update: Delaware, Illinois, Nebraska, New Jersey, Pennsylvania (Philadelphia), and Virginia  U.S. Hemp Roundtable

item.title: Some of Texas’ most famous BBQ joints forced to close due to sky-high beef prices - New York Post
item.description:
1. Some of Texas’ most famous BBQ joints forced to close due to sky-high beef prices  New York Post

item.title: SpaceX wins $2.29 billion Space Force contract for military data network - SpaceNews
item.description:
1. SpaceX wins $2.29 billion Space Force contract for military data network  SpaceNews
  2. SpaceX just won a $2 billion contract to make satellites for the Space Force  MarketWatch
  3. SpaceX wins $2.29B to speed Space Force’s LEO communications ‘backbone’  Breaking Defense
  4. US Space Force awards SpaceX $2.29 billion contract for military space data network  Reuters
  5. USSF Gives SpaceX $2.29 Billion for ‘Backbone’ of New Data Network  Air & Space Forces Magazine

item.title: Uber burned through its entire 2026 AI budget in four months. Now its COO is questioning whether it's worth it - Fortune
item.description:
1. Uber burned through its entire 2026 AI budget in four months. Now its COO is questioning whether it's worth it  Fortune
  2. Microsoft reports are exposing AI's real cost problem: Using the tech is more expensive than paying human employees  Fortune
  3. Uber's COO says it's getting harder to justify the money spent on AI tokenmaxxing  Business Insider
  4. If enough other companies report the same, the bubble pops. 🫧  Marcus on AI | Substack
  5. Uber president says AI spending is getting ‘harder to justify’  The Verge

item.title: Quantum Stocks Surge On U.S. Funding. Were Google, Microsoft, IonQ Snubbed? - Investor's Business Daily
item.description:
1. Quantum Stocks Surge On U.S. Funding. Were Google, Microsoft, IonQ Snubbed?  Investor's Business Daily
  2. Which company will the U.S. government take a stake in next? Here’s what traders think  CNBC
  3. Department of Commerce Announces Letters of Intent With 9 Companies for $2 Billion to Accelerate U.S. Leadership in Quantum Computing  National Institute of Standards and Technology (.gov)
  4. US’s big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal  Ars Technica
  5. IBM spins off America's first quantum chip foundry with $2 billion in federal and private funding — newly-minted 'Anderon' foundry to offer 300mm quantum wafer fab and manufacturing services  Tom's Hardware
